text: At equal L2 budget, targeted noise along the inter-class axis degrades nearest-centroid accuracy far more than random isotropic noise (37.3 vs 0.5 accuracy points at eps=1.0, d=32).
The synthetic testbed reproduces closed-form predictions (clean Phi(1), targeted Phi(1-eps)), so measured gaps are attributable to geometry, not harness artifacts.

A controlled synthetic testbed (two-Gaussian classes, nearest-centroid) where clean, random-noise, and targeted-noise accuracies have closed-form predictions, validating the harness before trusting it on learned models.
Measured accuracy-vs-eps curves for random vs targeted injection at equal L2 budget, quantifying the gap.
The targeted-vs-random gap grows with dimension d (random effective shift ~ eps/sqrt(d)).
The same protocol holds on a learned model (logistic regression -> small MLP) where the targeted direction is estimated from the model's own weights.
